rationale: Impacted-Domains parity holds. Both declared domains resolve to real OWNERSHIP files: .mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml (globs cmd/**, internal/panel/**, plugins/mindspec/**, .claude/workflows/**) and .mindspec/domains/core/OWNERSHIP.yaml (internal/config/**). Every write-touched path maps cleanly to a DECLARED domain: R1 cmd/mindspec/panel.go + internal/panel/{gate,create,panel}.go → workflow; R2 plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js + .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js → workflow; R3 same cmd + internal/panel → workflow; R4 internal/config → core. No touched path falls under an undeclared domain (context-system/execution). internal/instruct + internal/complete are fenced-unchanged and, if incidentally read, are also workflow (still declared). No mis-scope. Decomposition is clean: 4 requirements ↔ 4 already-assigned beads (4d9m/lczt/zw81/cthj), each self-contained with a runnable falsification clause and AC; no logical ordering dependency. R1 (P2, load-bearing) and R3 both edit cmd/mindspec/panel.go + internal/panel — merge-adjacency only, resolved trivially by sequential bead-cycle landing. Each requirement is scoped tightly enough for a one-round bead panel.
findings: PARITY: workflow + core OWNERSHIP.yaml both exist and glob-cover every touched path; spec's own attributions (internal/panel→workflow, internal/config→core) are accurate. No undeclared-domain leak. ADR touchpoints 0037/0035/0040/0034/0030 all resolve on disk. DECOMPOSITION: 4R→4 pre-assigned beads, independent, no hidden ordering dependency (R1 non-bead staleness vs R3 create-time gate stamping are orthogonal; R3 reads config resolvers read-only from cmd, no core edit). No requirement is secretly two beads: R1 rendering+fact-gathering+tally-recovery all cohere around one non-bead-staleness behavior; R3 flag+CreateInput.Gate+per-gate-default resolution cohere around one writer feature. No two beads should merge — each has a distinct falsification surface. MINOR (non-blocking): R1 and R3 both touch cmd/mindspec/panel.go and internal/panel, so parallel worktree landing risks textual merge conflict; sequential bead-cycle ordering (recommend R1 first as the P2 load-bearing fix) avoids it. git status clean apart from expected untracked reviews/ dir.
